name: Makaibari Tea Estate
address: Kurseong, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Established in 1859, Makaibari is one of the oldest tea estates in Darjeeling. A pioneer in biodynamic tea farming with a rich first-flush Muscatel character.

name: Tocklai Tea Estate
address: Cinnamara, Jorhat District, Assam
description: One of the most storied estates in the Brahmaputra Valley, producing bold malty CTC and orthodox teas. The estate adjoins the world-famous Tocklai Tea Research Centre.

name: Glendale Tea Estate
address: Coonoor, Nilgiris District, Tamil Nadu
description: A high-altitude Nilgiris estate producing year-round teas with a distinct muscatel note. Known for award-winning orthodox teas and direct estate sales.

name: Kangra Valley Tea Cooperative
address: Palampur, Kangra District, Himachal Pradesh
description: Himachal's sole significant tea-growing region, revived after near-extinction. Kangra teas have a delicate, light flavour unlike any other Indian variety.

name: Manjushree Tea Estate
address: Doom Dooma, Tinsukia District, Assam
description: Upper Assam estate producing some of the finest golden-tipped orthodox teas. Specialises in tippy golden flowery orange pekoe (TGFOP) grades.

name: Ambootia Tea Estate
address: North Kurseong, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Established between 1859–1864, among the first 30 tea gardens in Darjeeling. Located in north Kurseong at 950–1450 m elevation across 340 hectares. A 100% bio-organic and biodynamic estate since 1992, Fair Trade certified since 1994, and a UN FAO model farm for Darjeeling Organic Tea.

name: Puttabong (Tukvar) Tea Estate
address: 9 km North-East of Darjeeling Town, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Planted in 1852 by Dr Campbell, the first commercial tea plantation in Darjeeling and its largest at 476 hectares, stretching over 20 km to the Sikkim border. Owned by the Jayshree Tea Group. Set a world record auction price of Rs 10,001/kg for Antique Tea in 1992.

name: Singell Tea Estate
address: 2.5 km from Kurseong Town, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Established around 1871 by Mr. James White, one of Darjeeling's most historic estates celebrating 150 years in 2021. Converted to organic cultivation by the mid-1980s. Celebrated for bright herbal first flush and muscatel-rich second flush teas with Fairtrade and USDA certifications.

name: Badamtam Tea Estate
address: Lebong Valley, 17 km West of Darjeeling Town, West Bengal
description: First planted in 1858 by Christine Barnes, commercially started by Lebong Tea Company in the 1860s. Set in Lebong Valley at 305–1830 m elevation across 172 hectares. Offers magnificent views of Kanchenjunga. Features a 14-foot bronze Buddha statue. Owned by the Goodricke Group.

name: Gopaldhara Tea Estate
address: Rongbong Valley, Mirik, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Planted around 1881 in paddy fields owned by 'Gopal', with natural streams (dhara) running through it. One of the highest tea gardens in Darjeeling at 1500–2300 m elevation across 320 hectares. Managed by Gopaldhara Tea Company Pvt Ltd (Saria family). Famous for exceptional muscatel and specialty teas.

name: Arya Tea Estate
address: West Darjeeling, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Originally known as 'Sidrabong', renamed Arya in 1885 by Buddhist Monks who first cultivated Chinese tea here. Located in west Darjeeling at 900–1820 m elevation across 310 acres. Produces specialty teas named after gemstones: Ruby (Black), Emerald (Green), Pearl (White), Topaz (Oolong). Certified organic by IMO and JAS.

name: Goomtee Tea Estate
address: South Kurseong, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Originally planted by Henry Lennox in 1899, later owned by the Rana of Nepal and currently by the Kejriwal and Prasad families. Located in south Kurseong with 225 hectares at ~1220 m average elevation. Known for fine muscatel black teas processed to ISO 9001 and HACCP standards.

name: Monteviot Tea Estate
address: North Kurseong Valley, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A small organic tea estate located in the north Kurseong valley at 950–1700 m elevation across 88 hectares. Known for high-quality green Darjeeling teas. Part of the Lemongrass Organic Tea Estates Pvt Ltd portfolio. The factory was destroyed by fire in 2004 and subsequently rebuilt.

name: Selim Hill Tea Estate
address: Kurseong Subdivision, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A heritage Darjeeling tea estate in the Kurseong subdivision known for fine orthodox teas. Features picturesque slopes with views of the Himalayan foothills. Produces classic Darjeeling first and second flush teas with characteristic muscatel notes.

name: Barnesbeg Tea Estate
address: Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: One of the five Goodricke Group estates in Darjeeling, Barnesbeg is a high-altitude garden producing quality orthodox black and green Darjeeling teas. Situated at elevations offering views of the snow-capped Himalayan peaks. Known for consistent first flush and second flush quality.

name: Dilaram Tea Estate
address: Banks of River Rinchengtong, 8 km from Kurseong, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Established in 1890 by Mr Bell along the Rinchengtong river valley, 8 km from Kurseong. Tea cultivation spans 2200–5900 ft elevation. Part of Amgoorie India Limited under the Goodricke Group. Near the Bhanzang Salamander Lake, home to the endangered Himalayan Salamander.

name: Rohini Tea Estate
address: Southern Kurseong, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Located in south Kurseong at 1500–2300 m elevation across 138 hectares. Closed between 1962–1994, then revived by the Saria family who also own Gopaldhara. Features century-old original bushes in the upper section. Known for Imperial Emerald green tea.

name: Margaret's Hope Tea Estate
address: Northern Valley of Kurseong, P.O. Tung, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Named in memory of Margaret, daughter of owner Cruickshank who died returning from a visit in the 1920s. Located near Longview Highlands at 950–1830 m across 585 hectares. World-renowned for first flush, second flush and autumn teas. Abundant in Orchids. Owned by the Goodricke Group.

name: Upper Fagu Tea Estate
address: Kalimpong Sub-Division, West Bengal
description: Located in the Kalimpong sub-division, Upper Fagu is one of the estates officially designated under the Darjeeling Tea Geographical Indication. Situated at high altitude with classic Darjeeling terroir, it produces orthodox teas with floral and muscatel character.

name: Poobong Tea Estate
address: Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A high-altitude Darjeeling tea estate producing orthodox black teas with characteristic floral and delicate aroma. Situated in the rolling hills of Darjeeling district with classic Himalayan terroir that imparts the distinctive Darjeeling flavour profile.

name: Phuguri Tea Estate
address: Mirik Valley, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Located in the Mirik Valley across 432 total hectares with 282 hectares under tea cultivation. One of the larger estates producing 1,70,000 kg annually. Owned by the Bagaria Group. Produces black and green Darjeeling tea with old bushes replaced at 2% per year. Tea is plucked from March to June.

name: Giddapahar Tea Estate
address: Eagles Cliff, Kurseong, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Also known as Eagles Cliff, a 109-hectare garden perched on a cliff in Kurseong at an average altitude of 1482 m. A family-owned estate famed for producing exceptional Darjeeling green tea. Known for its Special Musk second flush and striking Himalayan mountain views from the garden.

name: Mim Tea Estate
address: Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A well-regarded Darjeeling tea estate producing organic black and green teas. Known for aromatic first flush teas with distinct astringency and a beautiful coppery liquor. Also produces a rare second flush green tea. Teas are exported internationally and have a loyal following in Europe.

name: Avongrove Tea Estate
address: Rungbong Valley, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A high-altitude organic tea estate in the Rungbong Valley at 2200–5500 ft elevation. Taken over by KPL International Group in 2008. About 60% China Clonal and 40% hybrid cultivars. Produces 60,000–70,000 kg of quality FTGFOP1 grade first flush teas annually.

name: Castleton Tea Estate
address: Hilly slopes of Kurseong and Pankhabari, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Formerly Kumseri, renamed Castleton after a castle-like building on the estate. Planted by Dr. Charles Graham in 1885. Located at 980–2300 m across 170 hectares. Consistently achieves world record auction prices for its prized Muscatel second flush. Garden sections bear colorful local names like Bhalu Khop and Dhobitar. Owned by the Goodricke Group.

name: North Tukvar Tea Estate
address: East Darjeeling, facing Kanchenjunga, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Located in east Darjeeling facing the Kanchenjunga massif. Covers 187 of 195 total hectares under tea at 1500–2800 ft elevation. Owned by Jay Shree Tea Company. Primarily produces green Darjeeling tea alongside Assam-China hybrids and clonal teas. Employs around 650 workers.

name: Makaibari Tea Estate
address: Kurseong, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Established in 1859, Makaibari set up the first tea processing factory in Darjeeling. 270 hectares under tea out of 750 total, with 65% natural forest. The first Darjeeling estate to practice organic farming. Owned for four generations by the Banerjee family. Famous for community home-stays and biodynamic practices.

name: Okayti Tea Estate
address: Mirik Valley, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Started in 1888 as Rungdoo Tea Garden, famously called 'the only okay tea' at London Tea Auctions, giving rise to the name Okayti. One of the highest average elevations of all Darjeeling estates at 4200–6000 ft. Located in Mirik Valley across 208 cultivated hectares with the Mechi river flowing through. Owned by the Kumbhat family.

name: Thurbo Tea Estate
address: Mirik Valley, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Planted in 1872. Name derived from 'Tombu' (local word for camp) referencing a British military base during the Nepal invasion. Located in Mirik valley at 760–1890 m across 485 hectares with the Mechi and Rangbang rivers flowing through. Owned by Goodricke Group (Camellia Plc).

name: Singbulli Tea Estate
address: Near Mirik on NH 35, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Tea processing factory started in 1924. Located near Mirik on NH 35 at 1200–4100 ft elevation across 474 hectares. Produces 250 tons of tea per year including black, green and specialty teas. Employs over 1325 workers. Jointly held by the BK Birla Group and Jayshree Tea.

name: Lopchu Tea Estate
address: Between Darjeeling Town and Teesta River, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Located between Darjeeling town and the Teesta river with 226 acres under tea. About 95% of the plantation consists of original China bushes. World-renowned for its distinct muscatel flavour and delicate aroma. Much of the produce is exported to the United States and European countries.

name: Balasun Tea Estate
address: Sonada, North Kurseong, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Established in 1871 and named after the Balasun river flowing through its base. Located in Sonada, north Kurseong at 365–1375 m elevation across 181 hectares. Tea composition is 51% pure China, 40% hybrid Assam, and 9% Darjeeling clonal. Owned by Jay Shree Tea Company since 2005.

name: Sungma Turzum Tea Estate
address: Rungbong Valley, 30 km South-West of Darjeeling Town, West Bengal
description: A combined estate 30 km south-west of Darjeeling in Rungbong Valley at 1155–1700 m across 282 hectares. Sungma is Tibetan for 'place of heavy mushroom growth'; Turzum means 'weekly village market'. The 1934 earthquake demolished Sungma's factory; processing now at Turzum. Produces 150 tons of black and green tea annually. Owned by Jayshree Tea Group.

name: Namring Tea Estate
address: Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Dating to 1855, one of Darjeeling's oldest and largest estates at 475+ hectares. Split into Upper and Lower divisions comprising Poomong, Jingiam, and Namring gardens with highest plantation at 1700 m. Famed for Muscatel teas from a blend of three-fifths China, three-tenths clonal, and one-tenth Assam hybrid cultivars. Managed by Darjeeling Impex.

name: Risheehat Lizahill Tea Estate
address: Eastern Valley, 15 km from Darjeeling Town, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: An organic estate with two divisions — Risheehat ('Home of the Saint', formerly Tsering Bagan) and Lizahill — established by British planters in the mid-1800s. Located 15 km from Darjeeling in the Eastern Valley with 256 hectares under tea. Annual production 120 tons including black, green, oolong, white and Silver Tips. Owned by Jayshree Tea Group.

name: Sourenee Tea Estate
address: Mirik Valley, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Founded in 1902 in the Mirik valley across 99 cultivated of 137 total hectares at 2500–5000 ft. Organic certified since April 2009 by Control Union. Known for the Black Blossom second flush black tea. Managed by Sourenee Leaves Pvt Ltd under Titagarh Wagons Ltd since 2007. A popular eco-tourism destination with a majestic bungalow.

name: Jungpana Tea Estate
address: P.O. Mahanadi, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Originally planted by Henry Montgomery Lennox, acquired by the Rana of Nepal post-WWII, then by the Kejriwal family since 1956. Located 10–12 km south of Kurseong at 1000–1500 m elevation, accessible only on foot via 380+ steep steps. World-famous for Muscatel teas sought by Fortnum & Mason, Harrods, and boutiques across Germany and Japan. Certified HACCP and JAS.

name: Glenburn Tea Estate
address: 1 hour from Darjeeling Town, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A heritage estate over 150 years old, owned by the Prakash family for four generations. Total area of 1600 acres with 390 acres under bio-organic tea. Elevation ranges from 3200 ft near the planter's bungalow down to 800 ft where two mountain rivers flow through. World-renowned for luxury tea tourism with heritage bungalow stays and premium green teas.

name: Mission Hill Tea Estate
address: Kalimpong Sub-Division, West Bengal
description: Located in the Kalimpong sub-division, Mission Hill is one of the estates officially designated under the Darjeeling Tea Geographical Indication. Situated at high altitude with classic Darjeeling terroir, producing quality orthodox teas with floral notes.

name: Happy Valley Tea Estate
address: 3 km from Darjeeling Town, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Established in 1854 by British planter David Wilson, with production starting by 1860. The closest tea estate to Darjeeling town at just 3 km. Over 270 acres of plantation. Entire production exported to international buyers including Harrods of London. Part of the Lemongrass Organic Tea Estates portfolio.

name: Aloobari Tea Estate
address: Aloobari, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A heritage Darjeeling tea estate named after the Aloobari area near Darjeeling town. Produces quality orthodox Darjeeling teas at high altitude. The estate is situated amidst scenic Himalayan landscape and contributes to the Darjeeling GI tea legacy.

name: Orange Valley Tea Estate
address: West Darjeeling, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Formerly Bloomfield Tea Garden, now owned by the Bagaria Group. Located in west Darjeeling at 5000–6000 ft elevation across 220 cultivated of 347 total hectares. Fringed by orange trees and retains century-old original China bushes. Produces 1,30,000 kg annually. First flush offers flowery China flavour; second flush has distinct muscatel.

name: Samabeong Tea Estate
address: Kalimpong Sub-Division, West Bengal
description: Located in the Kalimpong sub-division, Samabeong is one of the estates officially designated under the Darjeeling Tea Geographical Indication. Situated at high altitude producing quality orthodox Darjeeling teas with characteristic floral and muscatel notes.

name: Dhajea Tea Estate
address: Rungbong Valley, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Owned by the Chamong Group, established in the 1870s. Located in Rungbong Valley at an average altitude of 3000 ft across 180 cultivated of 317 total hectares. A bio-organic garden employing around 500 workers producing 95,000 kg annually. An on-site factory was set up in 1995 after cable-car transport of leaves was found to compromise tea flavour.

name: Bannockburn Tea Estate
address: West of Darjeeling Town, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Founded in the 1850s, named after the 1314 Battle of Bannockburn in Glasgow by Scottish planters who found the terrain reminiscent of their homeland. Located west of Darjeeling town at 1900–5600 ft across ~285 hectares. Part of the Chamong Group. Produces premium first flush Darjeeling teas.

name: Ging Tea Estate
address: Lebong Valley, West Darjeeling, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A bio-organic estate of the Chamong Group in Lebong Valley, west Darjeeling. The estate spans 255 hectares under tea out of 600+ total hectares, with 230 hectares as natural forest. Elevation ranges from 2000–5000 ft. Employs over 600 workers producing black, green and white Darjeeling teas.

name: Pussimbing Tea Estate
address: Ghoom area, below Tiger Hill, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A bio-organic garden of the Chamong Group located below Tiger Hill in Ghoom. 'Pussimbing' is Lepcha for 'place of streams', reflecting the many streams passing through. 201 hectares under tea at 1500–2200 m elevation. Produces 90,000 kg of black Darjeeling tea annually.

name: Nagri Farm Tea Estate
address: Rungbong Valley, South-West Darjeeling, West Bengal
description: Started in 1857 as a dairy farm by British owner Mr. Greenhill, fully converted to tea by 1883. Located in Rungbong Valley at 2400–6500 ft across 256 tea acres. The factory caught fire in 1912 and 1962 before being rebuilt. Taken over by the Chamong Group. Annual production ~1,90,000 kg employing over 900 workers.

name: Lingia Tea Estate
address: 30 km West of Darjeeling Town, near Nepal Border, West Bengal
description: Founded in 1867 by German missionaries J.A. and F.J. Wernicke. 'Lingia' is Lepcha for 'triangle of 8 peaks'. Pure China cultivar on 141 of 220 total hectares at 3000–6000 ft. Famed for a unique natural rose flavour unreplicable elsewhere in Darjeeling. Taken over by Chamong Group in 2001. Certified 100% bio-organic with USDA, NPOP, JAS, HACCP and Fair Trade accreditations.

name: Marybong Tea Estate
address: 12 km from Ghoom, West Darjeeling, West Bengal
description: Originally part of Lingia Tea Estate, gifted to the owner's daughter Mary at her marriage in the 1870s. 'Marybong' means 'Mary's Place' in Lepcha. Located 12 km from Ghoom at 3000–6000 ft across 285 tea hectares. Bio-dynamic garden of Chamong Group producing black, green and white teas. Annual production 1,70,000 kg.

name: Soom Tea Estate
address: Darjeeling Pulbazar, 18 km from Darjeeling Town, West Bengal
description: Planted in 1860 by Captain J. Jerdin. 'Soom' is Lepcha for 'triangle', reflecting the garden's triangular shape. Covers 237 planted hectares on the north-west side of Darjeeling, 18 km from town. Pure Chinary cultivar teas predominantly exported to Germany and Europe. High-altitude garden aiming for 100% organic conversion.

name: Takdah Tea Estate
address: Teesta Valley, Kalimpong District, West Bengal
description: Established in 1864, one of the early heritage gardens in the Kalimpong district's Teesta Valley at 2500–6500 ft across 288 hectares, 27 km from Darjeeling town. Produces first flush, second flush and autumn crop teas. Exports primarily to the UK, USA, Japan and Middle East.

name: Tumsong Tea Estate
address: Eastern Darjeeling, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A high-altitude Darjeeling estate in eastern Darjeeling producing teas with distinctive floral and delicate aroma. Part of the official Darjeeling GI designation. The estate contributes to the region's premium tea production with classic Himalayan terroir characteristics.

name: Longview Tea Estate
address: Pankhabari Road, Kurseong, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Opened by British planter C.G. Adams in 1873, owned by Wyandhams of Australia until 1953. Now owned by Longview Tea & Agro Ltd. Located along the Pankhabari road with 506 hectares under tea — the largest single-estate Darjeeling tea producer at ~10% of total output (0.7 million kg/year). Employs nearly 2000 workers. Factory was rebuilt after a devastating 2008 fire.

name: Kanchaan View Tea Estate
address: Rungeet Valley, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Also known as Rungeet Tea Estate, Kanchaan View is set in the Rungeet Valley with commanding views of the Kanchenjunga massif. Produces quality orthodox Darjeeling teas at high altitude with classic Himalayan terroir. A GI designated estate contributing to Darjeeling's premium tea heritage.

name: Chongtong Tea Garden
address: North of Lingia Tea Estate, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: A bio-organic tea garden in the western Darjeeling hills, north of Lingia Tea Estate. Part of the Lemongrass Organic Tea Estates Pvt Ltd portfolio. Produces quality orthodox Darjeeling teas at high altitude with characteristic floral and muscatel notes.

name: Phoobsering Tea Garden
address: West Darjeeling Valley, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: One of the oldest tea plantations in Darjeeling, named after its supervisor Phoob Tshering. Located in the west Darjeeling valley at 3000–6000 ft across 240 cultivated of 510 total hectares. A bio-organic farm of the Chamong Group specialising in delicate White Darjeeling Tea. Annual production 1,30,000 kg.

name: Ringtong Tea Garden
address: Kurseong Subdivision, Darjeeling District, West Bengal
description: Located west of Dilaram Tea Estate in the Kurseong subdivision along the banks of the Ringtong river. Produces quality orthodox Darjeeling teas at high altitude. A heritage garden contributing to the Kurseong north belt's premium tea legacy under the Darjeeling GI designation.

name: Powai Tea Estate
address: Powai Tea Estate, Tinsukia district, Assam, India
description: Powai Tea Estate is located in the Tinsukia district of Upper Assam, part of the Brahmaputra valley tea belt known for strong, malty Assam teas. The estate was previously owned by TATA Tea (bought in 1983) and was later transferred to Amalgamated plantations in 2007 as part of plantation restructuring. It continues to operate as a major CTC tea producing estate.

name: Dehing Tea Estate
address: P.O. Galai, Margherita region, Tinsukia district, Assam, India
description: Dehing Tea Estate is located in the easternmost tea-growing belt of Upper Assam near the Margherita region in Tinsukia district. Established in 1901, the estate is named after the Buri Dehing River and has historically been part of the  group. Spread across approximately 650 hectares with nearly 490 hectares under tea cultivation, the estate produces primarily CTC teas known for their strong, bright liquor. It lies within the high-rainfall Brahmaputra valley ecosystem, making it ideal for consistent high-volume tea production.
